current_state: The school treats the seven Schoolwide Learner Outcomes as single-word labels. Only Confident carries a published behavioral descriptor. Bilingual Communicators, Compassionate, Engaged, Innovative, Reflective, and Responsible appear on wall materials without shared descriptors. Unit plans in Math, English, Humanities, Sciences, Business and Economics, Art Department, and Music and Sports Department do not name the SLOs each unit advances. Assessment artifacts are not SLO-tagged. PLC cycles in Curriculum and Teaching, in the subject departments, and in the SAO - Student Affairs Office (Pastoral and Well-Being) do not open on an SLO frame or close on SLO-tagged evidence. Parent reporting from the Principal's Office (PO) and the SAO - Student Affairs Office (Pastoral and Well-Being) reports subject grades without an SLO-organized narrative. Students in IG1, IG2, AS, and A2 do not use SLO vocabulary in self-reflection at term reporting or portfolio review. Administration / Leadership data conversations rarely cite an SLO as the interpretive lens.
desired_state: Seven behavioral descriptors stand published at the depth of the current Confident descriptor, authored by cross-divisional faculty teams drawn from English, Math, Sciences, Humanities, Business and Economics, Art Department, and Music and Sports Department, validated by the IHS Principal, the Director of Teaching and Curriculum Center, the Director of Student Affairs Office, and the Director of AAO, and posted in the staff handbook, parent handbook, and student-facing materials. Every department's Term 2 unit plans name one or two SLOs per unit and carry at least one SLO-tagged assessment artifact. The unit-planning template and the gradebook carry SLO fields. PLC cycles in every division open on an SLO frame and close on SLO-tagged evidence in the minutes. Parent reporting at each cycle of 2025-2026 includes an SLO-organized narrative section for every enrolled student in IG1, IG2, AS, and A2 in addition to subject grades. Students submit an SLO self-reflection at term reporting and at portfolio review and name which SLOs a given piece of their work demonstrates. The annual self-study evidence binder against A1, A4, B1, B2, B3, and C1 is compiled with the seven SLOs as the organizing evidence categories, drawing on unit-level, PLC-level, student-reflection, and parent-reporting artifacts produced during the cycle.
rationale: The seven Schoolwide Learner Outcomes are the operational expression of the school's three mission areas: Academic excellence, Caring and safe community, and Global citizenship. The mission clauses GROUNDED, ROUNDED, and UNBOUNDED and the vision clauses on engagement, lifelong learning, personal responsibility, self-esteem, bilingual communication, and compassion are the source statements the SLOs render into daily classroom practice. Six of the seven SLOs lack a shared behavioral descriptor, so faculty in English, Math, Sciences, Humanities, Business and Economics, Art Department, and Music and Sports Department, and leadership in the Principal's Office (PO), the SAO - Student Affairs Office (Pastoral and Well-Being), Curriculum and Teaching, and the AAO - Academic Affairs Office cannot reference a common definition when planning, observing, assessing, or reporting. Standard A1 requires the mission and vision to be collectively understood and implemented; standard A4 requires data-driven continuous improvement; standards B1, B2, and B3 require curriculum, instruction, and assessment to respond to defined student outcomes; standard C1 requires a shared community vocabulary for student growth. Without shared SLO descriptors and SLO-tagged evidence, the school cannot produce audit-ready evidence against A1, A4, B1, B2, B3, or C1. Work in the areas Bilingual Environment, Curriculum and Learning, Faculty PD and PLC, School Culture, Student Data and Well-being, Community Involvement, and Resources and Tools is the path that closes the gap, and Administration / Leadership, Staff / Faculty, Students, Parents, and the Parent Committee are the stakeholder groups that must share the resulting vocabulary.
student_impact_framing: Students in IG1, IG2, AS, and A2 encounter the same SLO vocabulary in unit introductions in every department, in PLC-shaped instructional adjustments, in assessment artifacts, in term reports, and in portfolio review. Students name which SLOs a given piece of their work demonstrates and self-reflect against Bilingual Communicators, Compassionate, Confident, Engaged, Innovative, Reflective, and Responsible at term reporting and at portfolio review. Parents receive an SLO-organized narrative for their child at each reporting cycle alongside subject grades, so home conversations reference the same outcomes the classroom references. The Confident descriptor, which names risk-taking and idea expression as how growth happens, becomes the model students see extended to the other six outcomes, giving students a shared language for what learning looks like across English, Math, Sciences, Humanities, Business and Economics, Art Department, and Music and Sports Department.
provenance: The plan draws on the existing published Confident descriptor as the depth benchmark for the six descriptors to be authored. It draws on the mission clauses GROUNDED, ROUNDED, and UNBOUNDED and the six vision clauses as the source statements the SLOs operationalize. It draws on the current unit-plan repository, the gradebook, the PLC minutes, the term report template, the portfolio-review routine, the staff handbook, the parent handbook, and student-facing learning materials as the artifacts to be revised. It draws on the WASC standards A1, A4, B1, B2, B3, and C1 as the accreditation criteria the evidence binder addresses. It draws on the enumerated areas for improvement Bilingual Environment, Curriculum and Learning, Faculty PD and PLC, School Culture, Student Data and Well-being, Community Involvement, and Resources and Tools as the operational domains the work runs through. It draws on the divisional roster — the AAO - Academic Affairs Office, Art Department, Business and Economics, College Counseling Department, Curriculum and Teaching, English, Future Scholar Innovation Center, Humanities, Library, Math, Music and Sports Department, Principal's Office (PO), Recruitment and Admissions, SAO - Student Affairs Office (Pastoral and Well-Being), and Sciences — as the organizational units that carry the work.
